items.title: Gina Rinehart judgement looming in high-stakes battle over mining riches

children.content: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgement to be handed down on Wednesday in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.

thumbnail.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.

items.title: Dash cam video of near-misses on WA highways prompts road safety call

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

items.title: Man on trial for alleged assault of several women

children.content: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

image.altText: A medium shot with a sign reading: District Court of Western Australia.

items.title: Travellers shocked by suspension of WA tourist flights

children.content: Rising fuel costs due to the war in Iran see Qantas suspend Jetstar flights between Sydney and Busselton.

image.altText: Passengers arrive at Busselton Airport after the flight from Sydney.

items.title: WA government says it has secured 4 million litres of diesel

children.content: Four million litres of diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state.

gallery.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.

thumbnail.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine after worst season on record

image.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

items.title: Teenager 'begging' minister to deport abuser who had visa reinstated

children.content: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

image.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Trail mix and water help 87yo woman survive near-freezing night in dense forest

children.content: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

image.altText: A forest of giant karri trees, looking upwards through the trunks to the canopy

items.title: WA government eyes off unused COVID quarantine facility for prison

children.content: The WA government flags the possible conversion of the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre into a prison amid an "unprecedented" rise in the number of inmates being crammed into cells across the state.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a construction site

gallery.altText: Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

thumbnail.altText: Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

items.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in major holiday town

children.content: A homeless survey conducted in one of WA's best-known holiday hotspots uncovers dozens of people sleeping rough, including a 75-year-old.

gallery.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Vans and cars parked at Surfer's point during the day

thumbnail.altText: the main street in Margaret River with parked cars lined on either side of the street
Vans and cars parked at Surfer's point during the day

items.title: Massive hospital spend creates more questions than answers

children.content: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook wearing a white hard hat and yellow hi-ves vest speaks during a media conference at a construction site.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

children.content: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.

gallery.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

thumbnail.altText: A huge set of bush clad sandstone peninsula with a gap in the middle showing ocean rushing through

items.title: Safety alert issued amid explosion of illegal peptide use

children.content: The medicines regulator issues a health alert about the risks of using unregulated peptides amid a surge in the use of potentially unsafe injectables.

image.altText: A close up of a syringe and needle

items.title: Trump's 'AI Jesus' post sparks backlash as pope responds to attack

children.content: Donald Trump is criticised by religious sections of his supporter base after publishing, then deleting, the image, but the US president says: "I thought it was me as a doctor."

image.altText: A painting-style image showing Donald Trump in white robes resting his hand on the forehead of a hospital patient.

items.title: Mystery of seventh man unsolved, months after Chinese boat arrivals

children.content: Months after the arrival of six Chinese nationals at Kalumburu in Western Australia's far north, questions remain about another man suspected of attempting to assist them.

gallery.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

thumbnail.altText: a graphic of people on a boat

items.title: INXS is out, Phil Collins in as Rock & Roll Hall of Fame 2026 inductees announced

children.content: INXS has missed out on landing a place in the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame with 2026 inductees including Phil Collins, Queen Latifah and Fela Kuti.

image.altText: Phil Collins singing into a microphone, mouth wide open, arm up, pink and blue lights in background

items.title: Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds create comedy chaos in new show

children.content: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

image.altText: A woman in a red and blue costume leans on the bars of a wrestling rink

items.title: Shock loss of Emily has friends saying 'get your moles checked'

children.content: Emily avoided the sun and "did everything right". No-one considered that a melanoma removed from her shoulder in 2022 had spread to her brain.

gallery.altText: A group of people in black and purple clothing sit together on a bench. Shirts read "Remembering Em."
Two young women take a selfie together, both smiling at the camera, each with one hand under her chin.
Images of a young woman in glasses, smiling, painting a picture in a studio and holding a magazine at a table.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people in black and purple clothing sit together on a bench. Shirts read "Remembering Em."
Two young women take a selfie together, both smiling at the camera, each with one hand under her chin.
Images of a young woman in glasses, smiling, painting a picture in a studio and holding a magazine at a table.

items.title: How I've learnt to embrace the new normal of only having one breast

children.content: I decided to keep my healthy breast, opting against reconstruction. It means I'm now what's known in the community as a uniboober.

image.altText: Aarthi with her dog during chemo treatment

items.title: Debunking four myths about Australia's fuel crisis

children.content: If you listen to increasingly shrill calls, it is time to rebuild our oil industry. While the sentiments might be well-meant, a few unfortunate facts have been overlooked.

image.altText: Cars queued up for fuel at a Mobil petrol station in Port Moresby

items.title: Roblox to launch accounts for children after grooming claims

children.content: Roblox will launch restricted accounts for children and teenagers, two months after being put on notice by the government over reports children were being groomed on the platform.

image.altText: A boy poses for a photo while holding a smartphone in front of a screen.

items.title: Starc honoured as Wisden slams England's 'feckless' Ashes

children.content: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

image.altText: Australian fast bowler Mitchell Starc turns and smiles as he raises his arm in celebration after taking an Ashes wicket.

items.title: Tour operator cops $330,000 in cancellations as relief arrives for cyclone-hit businesses

children.content: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference" as Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

gallery.altText: A whale shark floats in clear water below a blue and white tour boat, with the name "Draw Card" on its bow.

thumbnail.altText: A whale shark floats in clear water below a blue and white tour boat, with the name "Draw Card" on its bow.
